rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope when binding implementation-level outputs or state.
Preserve AADL component type structure by not introducing implementation-specific references into type-level annexes.
generic_rules: Only reference features visible at the scope of the target component (type or implementation) in AGREE annexes.
Implementation annexes may contain assign statements for output bindings; component type annexes must avoid implementation-specific paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for all AGREE contracts.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, bool — never use AADL property names or package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly imported.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scope unless they are tied to assign statements.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or AADL properties as variables in AGREE expressions.
